# Transcrição: 27-How to Shift Creative Into the Conversion Campaign

- **Fonte:** 27-How to Shift Creative Into the Conversion Campaign
- **Duração:** 04:00
- **Idioma:** en

## [00:00 - 03:03]

Okay, so once I've figured out which creative is doing really well inside of the rapid fire test, what I do now is I want to move it into a conversion objective campaign. So I'm going to click on create and build that now. And again you're following pretty much the same naming conventions as what you've listed out inside of your funnel tracking sheet. So let's go here. We're going to pick a conversion objective and I'm just going to paste my naming convention in there. And we're going to use the saved audience that did well for us as well inside of our rapid fire test. So use feed, Instagram. We're going to optimize for purchases. Just say it was this one. And just make sure our placement's where we want it to be. And I'm going to give this $20 a day budget to start off with. And what we're going to do is click use existing ad. I've pulled up the ad inside the news feed so by clicking preview of the one that worked really well. And then I basically go in here and click use post ID. And again I'm going to use my UTM here. Paste that in there. And it should bring up this ad. So this is basically how you shift the audiences as well as the creative that worked really well into a conversion campaign. Now when you do that obviously you don't want to continue testing out creative to that same audience inside a post engagement objective. Because what's probably going to happen there is Facebook will prioritize your post engagement campaign and it potentially will cannibalize your conversion campaign. So when you have essentially moved your top performing personas and targeting as well as creative into a conversion I guess graduated or whatever term you want to use here into the stage two of your conversion campaign. What we want to do here is we want to go back into our post engagement our PPE and pause any audiences that we're now using inside of our conversion campaign so that it's not cannibalizing that conversion campaign. However you're still going to want to be testing out creative. This is an ongoing process if you want to run campaigns really well in Facebook you need to constantly be testing out new creative so that you don't get cut off guard when you start to see ad fatigue happening in your campaigns and also so that you can actually optimize for the best result. So if that's the case if you're going

## [03:03 - 04:00]

to be rapid fire testing creative which you can also do inside of your conversion campaign as well. But if you're going to rapid fire test creative inside of your post engagement campaign what I would recommend is you actually run an audience of people that visited the website say you know 60 days ago so they're outside of your retargeting stack they're no longer getting targeted by your retargeting but however they were you know potentially a viable lead they visited the website so I would target either something like that or I would target a longer look alike so a 2 or 3% look alike. So you can look at other variations of audiences to target at the stage as well and if you had no previous data hopefully after your rapid fire test you do have enough hits to that website to build a look alike from so you will hopefully have some options.
